episode.title: Le feu rouge 🎧
episode.source: Real Life French — Choses à Savoir

transcript.speaker: Julien
transcript.fr: Pff, je suis vraiment dégoûté. Je viens de recevoir une amende parce que j'ai grillé un feu rouge il y a 3 semaines sans faire exprès.
transcript.en: I'm really disgusted. I just got a fine for running a red light 3 weeks ago by accident.

transcript.speaker: Louise
transcript.fr: Oh là là, faut vraiment que tu sois plus attentif au volant ! Vaut mieux ça que d’avoir un accident, ceci dit !
transcript.en: You really need to be more careful driving! It's better than having an accident, that's for sure.

transcript.speaker: Julien
transcript.fr: Oui, t’as raison, mais la facture pique un peu. Surtout que j’étais persuadé d’être passé à l’orange… J’aurais dû être plus vigilant.
transcript.en: Yes, you're right, but the bill is a bit of a sting. Especially since I was sure I went through on orange... I should have been more careful.

transcript.speaker: Louise
transcript.fr: Ça te servira de leçon pour la prochaine fois. En attendant, prends le vélo pour te rendre au boulot, au moins tu seras moins un danger public !
transcript.en: It'll serve as a lesson for next time. In the meantime, take your bike to work, at least you'll be less of a public danger!

cards.fr: Je viens de recevoir une amende
cards.note: Expression used to talk about receiving a penalty, often for a minor infraction.

cards.fr: Faut vraiment que tu sois plus attentif
cards.en: You really need to be more careful
cards.note: Imperative sentence structure to give advice or orders.

cards.en: I should have been more careful
cards.note: Use of the conditional tense to express regret or past obligation.
Idiomatic expression to talk about learning from a mistake.

cards.fr: Prends le vélo pour te rendre au boulot
cards.note: Imperative sentence structure to give advice or orders, with a focus on transportation.

cards.fr: Tu seras moins un danger public
cards.en: You'll be less of a public danger
cards.note: Expression used to talk about being a risk to others, often in a humorous or ironic way.

cards.fr: Vaut mieux ça que d’avoir un accident
cards.en: It's better than having an accident
cards.note: Expression used to compare two situations and choose the less undesirable option.
